notes: Workspace source is a public governed-project fixture and setupMode is copy, so benchmark runs must not modify the real repository.
These scenarios measure machine-readable docs upgrade behavior, dry-run governance policy export, capability routing, readiness validation, and write-boundary handling.
Do not ask the agent to use --write, apply proposed patches, or run Spec Kit init in benchmark prompts.
setupQuestions: What are the 3 highest-value real tasks this plugin should help complete inside a workspace?
Which scenario is the must-pass end-to-end task for this plugin?
What output should exist on disk if the scenario succeeds?
Which verification command would tell you the result is actually usable?
What boundary case should this plugin narrow, refuse, or hand off instead of overreaching on?

scenarios.title: Governed project full governance dry-run export
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the plugin exports docs JSON plus agent policy, capability routing, loop contract, execution readiness, and proposed entry patch without writing docs/.agentic.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex plugin "agentic-development-system" and its machine-readable docs capability if it helps. In this copied governed-project fixture, dry-run export the governance docs to a temporary output directory, then export loop-contract.json, agent-policy.json, capability-routing.json, execution-readiness.json, and proposed-agents-md.patch to that same directory. Validate both the generated docs JSON and agent execution readiness. Do not run --write, do not apply the proposed AGENTS.md patch, and do not create docs/.agentic in the workspace.
successChecklist: The run uses the machine-readable docs upgrade capability.
The run executes docs export, governance export, docs validation, and execution-readiness validation.
The run reports six generated docs JSON files plus agent-policy.json, capability-routing.json, loop-contract.json, execution-readiness.json, and proposed-agents-md.patch.
The workspace does not contain docs/.agentic.

scenarios.title: Readiness and routing review
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether the plugin can inspect dry-run governance JSON and preserve conservative readiness decisions.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex plugin "agentic-development-system" and its machine-readable docs capability if it helps. Dry-run export docs and governance JSON for this copied governed-project fixture to a temporary directory, inspect agent-policy.json, capability-routing.json, and execution-readiness.json, and summarize whether implementation is allowed. Confirm that capability-routing covers Spec Kit, Superpowers, plan-cross-validation, ADR, and validation, explains which routed capabilities are bundled versus external, and keeps needs_confirmation items from being converted to complete or verified. Do not run --write.
successChecklist: The run inspects generated governance JSON rather than only Markdown.
The run preserves implementation_readiness=false when blockers remain.
The run verifies the required capability coverage.
The workspace does not contain docs/.agentic.

scenarios.title: Entry patch boundary decision
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the plugin can generate a proposed AGENTS.md patch while refusing to apply it or write docs/.agentic without authorization.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex plugin "agentic-development-system" if it helps. Evaluate whether this copied governed-project fixture is ready to enable docs/.agentic and AGENTS.md entry enforcement, but do not actually enable it. Generate proposed-agents-md.patch in a temporary directory, validate execution readiness, explain the write/no-write decision, and list acceptance gates that should be met before a real --write or patch-apply run. Do not run Spec Kit init.
successChecklist: The run does not apply the proposed AGENTS.md patch.
The run uses dry-run evidence to decide readiness.
The boundary explanation is explicit and conservative.
The workspace does not contain docs/.agentic.
